transport.h(3) Library Functions Manual transport.h(3)

NAME

transport.h - Transport abstraction layer for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acip_transport_methods_t
Transport method table (virtual function table). struct acip_transport
Transport instance structure.

Enumerations


enum acip_transport_type_t { ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP = 1, ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET = 2, ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBRTC = 3, ACIP_TRANSPORT_HTTP = 4, ACIP_TRANSPORT_QUIC = 5 }
Transport type enumeration.

Functions


void acip_transport_destroy (acip_transport_t *transport)
Destroy transport and free all resources. acip_transport_t * acip_tcp_transport_create (const char *name, socket_t sockfd, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create TCP transport from existing socket. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. acip_transport_t * acip_webrtc_transport_create (struct webrtc_peer_connection *peer_conn, struct webrtc_data_channel *data_channel, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)

Detailed Description

Transport abstraction layer for ACIP protocol.

Provides a transport-agnostic interface that allows ACIP protocol handlers to work with any underlying transport (TCP, WebSocket, HTTP, etc.).

Transport Layer Design

Architecture

The transport layer abstracts protocol delivery, allowing the same ACIP protocol handlers to work with multiple underlying transports:

  • TCP (acip_tcp_transport_create): Raw TCP sockets, binary packet streaming
  • WebSocket (acip_websocket_client_transport_create): HTTP upgrade, frame-based
  • WebRTC (acip_webrtc_transport_create): P2P data channels, encryption at transport
  • HTTP (future): Long-polling, REST-style
  • QUIC (future): UDP with congestion control

Design Principles

1.
Protocol code never calls socket()/send()/recv() directly
2.
Same ACIP handlers work with any transport implementation
3.
Each transport handles its own connection state and reliability
4.
Clean separation between protocol logic (acip_*) and transport logic

Packet Framing

Transports handle packet framing transparently:

  • TCP: Uses packet_header_t (magic + type + length + CRC32 + client_id)
  • WebSocket: Wraps packets in WebSocket frames
  • WebRTC: Sends packets directly on data channel

Version Information

  • Transport API Version: 1.0 (stable)
  • Supported ACIP Version: 1.0
  • Release Date: January 2026

DESIGN GOALS:

1.
Protocol code never calls socket()/send()/recv() directly
2.
Same ACIP handlers work with TCP, WebSocket, or any future transport
3.
Transport implementations handle their own connection state
4.
Clean separation between protocol logic and transport logic

USAGE PATTERN: 

// Create TCP transport from existing socket
acip_transport_t *tcp = acip_tcp_transport_create(sockfd, crypto_ctx);
// Send packet with type-specific helper
asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_ascii_frame(tcp, frame_data, frame_size, ...);
// Receive packet with dispatcher
acip_client_receive_and_dispatch(tcp, &callbacks);
// Or create WebSocket client transport
acip_transport_t *ws = acip_websocket_client_transport_create("ws://localhost:27225", crypto_ctx);
asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_ascii_frame(ws, frame_data, frame_size, ...);
// Cleanup
acip_transport_destroy(tcp);
acip_transport_destroy(ws);

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport owns its connection state (socket, pointers, etc.)
  • send() does NOT take ownership of data (caller must keep it valid)
  • recv() allocates buffer, caller must free via out_allocated_buffer
  • destroy() cleans up all transport resources and frees transport structure

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Version

1.0 (Transport API)

Definition in file transport.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum acip_transport_type_t

Transport type enumeration. Identifies which transport implementation is being used. Useful for debugging and conditional logic.

Enumerator

ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP
Raw TCP socket.
ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET
WebSocket over TCP.
ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBRTC
WebRTC DataChannel (P2P).
ACIP_TRANSPORT_HTTP
HTTP long-polling (future).
ACIP_TRANSPORT_QUIC
QUIC/UDP (future).

Definition at line 93 of file transport.h.

93              {
94   ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP = 1,       
95   ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET = 2, 
96   ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBRTC = 3,    
97   ACIP_TRANSPORT_HTTP = 4,      
98   ACIP_TRANSPORT_QUIC = 5       
99 } acip_transport_type_t;










Function
  Documentation




acip_transport_t * acip_tcp_transport_create (const char *
  name, socket_t sockfd, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create TCP transport from existing socket.


Parameters


name Debug name for tracking this transport
  (required, e.g., "transport_client_5")


sockfd Connected socket descriptor


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context (may be NULL)


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Note


Caller retains socket ownership (transport doesn't close
  on destroy)


Definition at line 344 of file tcp/transport.c.


344                                                                                                              {
345   if (!name) {
346     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
347     return NULL;
348   }
349 
350   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
351     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ INVALID_SOCKET: sockfd=%d", sockfd);
352     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket descriptor");
353     return NULL;
354   }
355 
356   log_info("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 CREATE_START: sockfd=%d, crypto=%s", sockfd, crypto_ctx ? "yes" : "no");
357 
358   // Allocate transport structure
359   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
360   if (!transport) {
361     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ ALLOC_TRANSPORT_FAILED: size=%zu", sizeof(acip_transport_t));
362     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate TCP transport");
363     return NULL;
364   }
365 
366   log_debug("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TRANSPORT_ALLOCATED: transport=%p", (void *)transport);
367 
368   // Allocate TCP-specific data
369   tcp_transport_data_t *tcp_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(tcp_transport_data_t), tcp_transport_data_t *);
370   if (!tcp_data) {
371     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ ALLOC_TCP_DATA_FAILED: size=%zu", sizeof(tcp_transport_data_t));
372     SAFE_FREE(transport);
373     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate TCP transport data");
374     return NULL;
375   }
376 
377   log_debug("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TCP_DATA_ALLOCATED: tcp_data=%p", (void *)tcp_data);
378 
379   // Initialize TCP data
380   tcp_data->sockfd = sockfd;
381   tcp_data->is_connected = true;
382 
383   // Initialize send mutex to protect concurrent sends from multiple threads
384   int mutex_result = mutex_init(&tcp_data->send_mutex, "tcp_send");
385   if (mutex_result != 0) {
386     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ MUTEX_INIT_FAILED: result=%d", mutex_result);
387     SAFE_FREE(tcp_data);
388     SAFE_FREE(transport);
389     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
390     return NULL;
391   }
392 
393   log_info("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TCP_DATA_INITIALIZED: sockfd=%d, is_connected=true, send_mutex=initialized", sockfd);
394 
395   // Enable TCP_NODELAY to disable Nagle's algorithm
396   // This ensures small packets are sent immediately instead of being buffered
397   int nodelay = 1;
398   if (setsockopt(sockfd, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_NODELAY, (const char *)&nodelay, sizeof(nodelay)) < 0) {
399     log_warn("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ⚠️  TCP_NODELAY_FAILED: sockfd=%d, %s", sockfd, SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
400     // Continue anyway - this is not fatal
401   } else {
402     log_debug("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TCP_NODELAY_SET: sockfd=%d", sockfd);
403   }
404 
405   // Initialize transport
406   transport->methods = &tcp_methods;
407   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
408   transport->impl_data = tcp_data;
409 
410   log_info("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ✅ CREATE_COMPLETE: transport=%p, sockfd=%d, is_connected=true, crypto=%s",
411            (void *)transport, sockfd, crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
412 
413   // Register transport
414   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
415 
416   // Register impl_data with transport as parent
417   NAMED_REGISTER(tcp_data, "impl", "tcp_impl", "0x%tx", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
418 
419   return transport;
420 }






References acip_transport::crypto_ctx, errno,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    acip_transport::impl_data, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    tcp_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_init(), NAMED_REGISTER,
    NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_STRERROR, tcp_transport_data_t::send_mutex,
    SET_ERRNO, and tcp_transport_data_t::sockfd.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(), add_client(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), server_connection_establish(), and
    server_main().






void
  acip_transport_destroy (acip_transport_t * transport)


Destroy transport and free all resources.


Parameters


transport Transport to destroy (may be NULL)


Note


Calls close() first if still connected



Frees the transport structure itself




Safe to pass NULL




Definition at line 426 of file tcp/transport.c.


426                                                          {
427   if (!transport) {
428     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ⚠️  NULL_TRANSPORT: nothing to destroy");
429     return;
430   }
431 
432   log_warn("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔴 DESTROY_START: transport=%p, impl_data=%p", (void *)transport, transport->impl_data);
433 
434   // Get type before we destroy for logging
435   acip_transport_type_t type = 0;
436   if (transport->methods && transport->methods->get_type) {
437     type = transport->methods->get_type(transport);
438     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 📋 TRANSPORT_TYPE: type=%d (1=TCP, 2=WebSocket, 3=WebRTC, ...)", type);
439   }
440 
441   // Close if still connected
442   if (transport->methods && transport->methods->close && transport->methods->is_connected &&
443       transport->methods->is_connected(transport)) {
444     log_warn("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔴 STILL_CONNECTED: calling close() first (type=%d)", type);
445     asciichat_error_t close_result = transport->methods->close(transport);
446     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔴 CLOSE_CALLED: result=%d", close_result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? -1 : 0);
447   } else {
448     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ ALREADY_CLOSED: skipping close (type=%d)", type);
449   }
450 
451   // Call custom destroy implementation if provided
452   if (transport->methods && transport->methods->destroy_impl) {
453     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔧 CALLING_DESTROY_IMPL: type=%d", type);
454     transport->methods->destroy_impl(transport);
455     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ DESTROY_IMPL_COMPLETE: type=%d", type);
456   } else {
457     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ⏭️  NO_CUSTOM_DESTROY: type=%d (using generic cleanup)", type);
458   }
459 
460   // Free implementation data
461   if (transport->impl_data) {
462     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🗑️  FREEING_IMPL_DATA: %p", transport->impl_data);
463     NAMED_UNREGISTER(transport->impl_data);
464     SAFE_FREE(transport->impl_data);
465     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ IMPL_DATA_FREED");
466   }
467 
468   // Free transport structure
469   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🗑️  FREEING_TRANSPORT: %p", (void *)transport);
470   SAFE_FREE(transport);
471 
472   log_warn("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ DESTROY_COMPLETE: type=%d", type);
473 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    acip_transport_methods_t::close,
    acip_transport_methods_t::destroy_impl,
    acip_transport_methods_t::get_type, acip_transport::impl_data,
    acip_transport_methods_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, log_warn, acip_transport::methods,
    NAMED_UNREGISTER, and SAFE_FREE.

    connection_attempt_tcp(), connection_context_cleanup(),
    discovery_session_destroy(), remove_client(),
    server_connection_close(), server_connection_establish(),
    server_connection_set_transport(), and server_main().






acip_transport_t * acip_webrtc_transport_create (struct
  webrtc_peer_connection * peer_conn, struct webrtc_data_channel *
  data_channel, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Definition at line 368 of file
  webrtc/transport.c.


369                                                                              {
370   if (!peer_conn || !data_channel) {
371     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "peer_conn and data_channel are required");
372     return NULL;
373   }
374 
375   // Allocate transport structure
376   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
377   if (!transport) {
378     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebRTC transport");
379     return NULL;
380   }
381 
382   // Allocate WebRTC-specific data
383   webrtc_transport_data_t *wrtc_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(webrtc_transport_data_t), webrtc_transport_data_t *);
384   if (!wrtc_data) {
385     SAFE_FREE(transport);
386     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebRTC transport data");
387     return NULL;
388   }
389 
390   // Create receive queue
391   wrtc_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(webrtc_recv_msg_t), WEBRTC_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
392   if (!wrtc_data->recv_queue) {
393     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
394     SAFE_FREE(transport);
395     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
396     return NULL;
397   }
398 
399   // Initialize synchronization primitives
400   if (mutex_init(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex, "webrtc_queue") != 0) {
401     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
402     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
403     SAFE_FREE(transport);
404     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize queue mutex");
405     return NULL;
406   }
407 
408   if (cond_init(&wrtc_data->queue_cond, "queue") != 0) {
409     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex);
410     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
411     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
412     SAFE_FREE(transport);
413     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize queue condition variable");
414     return NULL;
415   }
416 
417   if (mutex_init(&wrtc_data->state_mutex, "webrtc_state") != 0) {
418     cond_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_cond);
419     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex);
420     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
421     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
422     SAFE_FREE(transport);
423     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
424     return NULL;
425   }
426 
427   // Initialize WebRTC data
428   wrtc_data->peer_conn = peer_conn;
429   wrtc_data->data_channel = data_channel;
430   wrtc_data->is_connected = false; // Will be set to true in on_open callback
431 
432   // Register DataChannel callbacks
433   webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t callbacks = {
434       .on_open = webrtc_on_open,
435       .on_close = webrtc_on_close,
436       .on_error = webrtc_on_error,
437       .on_message = webrtc_on_message,
438       .user_data = wrtc_data,
439   };
440 
441   asciichat_error_t result = webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks(data_channel, &callbacks);
442   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
443     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->state_mutex);
444     cond_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_cond);
445     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex);
446     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
447     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
448     SAFE_FREE(transport);
449     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to set DataChannel callbacks");
450     return NULL;
451   }
452 
453   // IMPORTANT: The transport is always created from peer_manager's on_datachannel_open callback,
454   // which means the DataChannel is ALREADY OPEN when we get here. However, by setting our own
455   // callbacks above (webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks), we replaced the callbacks that would
456   // have set dc->is_open=true. So we need to manually mark both the transport AND the DataChannel
457   // as open/connected now.
458   //
459   // We cannot rely on webrtc_on_open being called later because:
460   // 1. The DataChannel is already open
461   // 2. libdatachannel won't fire the open event again
462   // 3. Setting callbacks after open doesn't trigger a retroactive open event
463 
464   // Mark DataChannel as open (needed for webrtc_datachannel_send() check)
465   webrtc_datachannel_set_open_state(data_channel, true);
466 
467   // Mark transport as connected
468   mutex_lock(&wrtc_data->state_mutex);
469   wrtc_data->is_connected = true;
470   mutex_unlock(&wrtc_data->state_mutex);
471   log_debug("Transport and DataChannel marked as connected/open (already open from peer_manager callback)");
472 
473   // Initialize transport
474   transport->methods = &webrtc_methods;
475   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
476   transport->impl_data = wrtc_data;
477 
478   log_info("Created WebRTC transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
479 
480   // Register WebRTC transport implementation data with transport as parent
481   NAMED_REGISTER(wrtc_data, "impl", "webrtc_impl", "0x%tx", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
482 
483   return transport;
484 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, cond_destroy(),
    cond_init(), acip_transport::crypto_ctx,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::data_channel, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    acip_transport::impl_data,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_init(), mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NAMED_REGISTER, webrtc_transport_data_t::peer_conn,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::queue_cond,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::queue_mutex,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, webrtc_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks(),
    webrtc_datachannel_set_open_state(), and
    WEBRTC_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE.






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create
  (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t *
  crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket client transport. Connects to a WebSocket server
    at the specified URL and creates a transport for sending/receiving ACIP
    packets over the WebSocket connection.




Parameters


name Debug name for tracking this transport
  (required, e.g., "transport_websocket_client")


url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225" or
  "wss://example.com/path")


crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on failure


Note


The URL must include the protocol (ws:// or wss://)



Port defaults to 80 for ws:// and 443 for wss:// if not
  specified




Connection is established synchronously during creation




Parameters


url WebSocket URL (e.g.,
  "ws://localhost:27225")


crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on failure


Definition at line 1395 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1396                                                                                        {
1397   if (!name) {
1398     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1399     return NULL;
1400   }
1401 
1402   if (!url) {
1403     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1404     return NULL;
1405   }
1406 
1407   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1408   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1409   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1410   if (!protocol_end) {
1411     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1412     return NULL;
1413   }
1414 
1415   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1416   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1417 
1418   // Find port (if specified)
1419   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1420   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1421 
1422   char host[256] = {0};
1423   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1424   char path[256] = "/";
1425 
1426   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1427     // Port is specified
1428     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1429     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1430       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1431       return NULL;
1432     }
1433     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1434     host[host_len] = '\0';
1435 
1436     // Extract port
1437     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1438     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1439       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1440       return NULL;
1441     }
1442   } else {
1443     // No port specified, use default
1444     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1445     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1446       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1447       return NULL;
1448     }
1449     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1450     host[host_len] = '\0';
1451   }
1452 
1453   // Extract path
1454   if (path_start) {
1455     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1456     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1457   }
1458 
1459   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1460 
1461   // Allocate transport structure
1462   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1463   if (!transport) {
1464     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1465     return NULL;
1466   }
1467 
1468   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1469   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1470       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1471   if (!ws_data) {
1472     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1473     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1474     return NULL;
1475   }
1476 
1477   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1478   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1479 
1480   // Create receive queue
1481   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1482   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1483     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1484     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1485     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1486     return NULL;
1487   }
1488 
1489   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1490   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1491   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1492     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1493     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1494     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1495     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1496     return NULL;
1497   }
1498 
1499   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1500   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1501   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1502   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1503 
1504   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1505     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1506     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1507     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1508     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1509     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1510     return NULL;
1511   }
1512 
1513   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1514     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1515     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1516     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1517     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1518     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1519     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1520     return NULL;
1521   }
1522 
1523   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1524     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1525     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1526     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1527     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1528     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1529     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1530     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1531     return NULL;
1532   }
1533 
1534   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1535     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1536     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1537     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1538     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1539     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1540     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1541     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1542     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1543     return NULL;
1544   }
1545 
1546   // Allocate initial send buffer
1547   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1548   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1549   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1550     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1551     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1552     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1553     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1554     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1555     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1556     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1557     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1558     return NULL;
1559   }
1560 
1561   // Create libwebsockets context
1562   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1563   // Register "http" protocol for WebSocket connections - matches server-side protocol name
1564   // This ensures libwebsockets will invoke our websocket_callback during upgrade handshake and communication
1565   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1566       {
1567           "http", // Default HTTP protocol - handles WebSocket upgrade and ACIP communication
1568           websocket_callback,
1569           0,      // Per-session data - using connect_info.userdata instead
1570           524288, // RX buffer size
1571           0,      // ID
1572           NULL,   // User pointer (set from connect_info.userdata)
1573           524288  // TX packet size
1574       },
1575       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1576   };
1577 
1578   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1579   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1580   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1581   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1582   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1583   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1584   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1585 
1586   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1587   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1588   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1589   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1590   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1591   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1592   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1593   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1594   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1595   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1596 
1597   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1598   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1599   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1600   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1601 
1602   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1603   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1604   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1605       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1606       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1607   };
1608   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1609 
1610   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1611   lws_log_init_client();
1612 
1613   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1614 
1615   if (!ws_data->context) {
1616     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1617     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1618     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1619     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1620     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1621     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1622     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1623     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1624     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1625     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1626     return NULL;
1627   }
1628 
1629   // Initialize connection state flags BEFORE calling lws_client_connect_via_info
1630   // The callback might fire immediately, so all fields must be ready
1631   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1632   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1633   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1634 
1635   // Connect to WebSocket server
1636   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1637   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1638   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1639   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1640   connect_info.address = host;
1641   connect_info.port = port;
1642   connect_info.path = path;
1643   connect_info.host = host;
1644   connect_info.origin = host;
1645   // Use default protocol - libwebsockets will use first protocol in array (our "http" protocol)
1646   // Don't specify local_protocol_name or protocol - let libwebsockets handle automatically
1647   connect_info.local_protocol_name = NULL; // Use default (first protocol in array)
1648   connect_info.protocol = NULL;            // No subprotocol - let libwebsockets choose automatically
1649   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1650   connect_info.ssl_connection =
1651       use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1652   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1653 
1654   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1655   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1656   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1657   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1658     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1659     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1660     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1661     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1662     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1663     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1664     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1665     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1666     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1667     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1668     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1669     return NULL;
1670   }
1671 
1672   // Initialize transport
1673   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1674   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1675   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1676 
1677   // Start service thread immediately - it will drive TLS handshake with frequent polling
1678   // The service thread checks is_destroying and context validity before calling lws_service,
1679   // so it's safe to start it right away without delays.
1680   ws_data->service_running = true;
1681   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1682     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1683     ws_data->service_running = false;
1684     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1685     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1686     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1687     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1688     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1689     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1690     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1691     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1692     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1693     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1694     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1695     return NULL;
1696   }
1697   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1698 
1699   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1700   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1701   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1702   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1703   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1704   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1705 
1706   // Register transport
1707   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1708 
1709   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1710   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1711 
1712   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1713   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1714   return transport;
1715 }






References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), cond_destroy(), cond_init(),
    websocket_transport_data_t::connection_failed,
    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_NETWORK, acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_destroying, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, lws_log_init_client(),
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT, NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_running,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_thread, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t *
  crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming
  clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1727 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1728                                                                                        {
1729   if (!name) {
1730     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1731     return NULL;
1732   }
1733 
1734   if (!wsi) {
1735     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1736     return NULL;
1737   }
1738 
1739   // Allocate transport structure
1740   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1741   if (!transport) {
1742     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1743     return NULL;
1744   }
1745 
1746   // Allocate transport-specific data
1747   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1748       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1749   if (!ws_data) {
1750     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1751     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1752     return NULL;
1753   }
1754 
1755   // Initialize receive queue
1756   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1757   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1758     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1759     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1760     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1761     return NULL;
1762   }
1763 
1764   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1765   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1766   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1767     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1768     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1769     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1770     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1771     return NULL;
1772   }
1773 
1774   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1775   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1776   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1777   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1778   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1779     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1780     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1781     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1782     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1783     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1784     return NULL;
1785   }
1786 
1787   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1788   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1789   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1790   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1791   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1792   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1793 
1794   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1795     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1796     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1797     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1798     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1799     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1800     return NULL;
1801   }
1802 
1803   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1804     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1805     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1806     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1807     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1808     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1809     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1810     return NULL;
1811   }
1812 
1813   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1814     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1815     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1816     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1817     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1818     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1819     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1820     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1821     return NULL;
1822   }
1823 
1824   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1825     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1826     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1827     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1828     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1829     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1830     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1831     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1832     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1833     return NULL;
1834   }
1835 
1836   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1837     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1838     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1839     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1840     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1841     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1842     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1843     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1844     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1845     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1846     return NULL;
1847   }
1848 
1849   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1850     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1851     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1852     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1853     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1854     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1855     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1856     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1857     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1858     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1859     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1860     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1861     return NULL;
1862   }
1863 
1864   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1865   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1866   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1867   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1868     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1869     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1870     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1871     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1872     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1873     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1874     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1875     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1876     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1877     return NULL;
1878   }
1879   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1880 
1881   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1882   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1883   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1884   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1885   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1886   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1887            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1888   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1889 
1890   // Initialize transport
1891   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1892   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1893   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1894 
1895   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1896 
1897   // Register transport
1898   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1899 
1900   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1901   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1902 
1903   return transport;
1904 }

    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT,
    NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.19
    ascii-chat
